vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-2181: extend boot-reconciliation grace default to 120s and restyle BootReconciliationModal to theme tokens (light/dark).

plan.title: Boot-reconciliation grace countdown: extend to 2 min + restyle modal to style-guide tokens (light/dark)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: The PAN-2076 boot-reconciliation gate shows the operator a countdown before all stopped work agents auto-resume on a dashboard boot. Two problems. (1) The grace default is 30 seconds, too short to react. The issue body cites only the fallback constant src/lib/cloister/boot-reconciliation.ts:14, but getBootReconciliationGraceSeconds() (boot-reconciliation.ts:40-45) reads loadCloisterConfigSync().startup.reconciliation_grace_secs FIRST and only falls back to the constant when that is missing/<=0. The effective default lives in DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG at src/lib/cloister/config.ts:289 (reconciliation_grace_secs: 30), which always wins. So changing only the constant would NOT change observed behavior — both sites plus the tests/lib/cloister/config.test.ts:142 assertion (toBe(30)) must change. (2) src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/BootReconciliationModal.tsx is hardcoded to a dark palette (bg-neutral-950, text-neutral-*, text-orange-*, bg-orange-*, bg-emerald-*, bg-zinc-*, bg-sky-*, bg-red-*), violating the style guide (STYLE-GUIDE.md:153 — no hardcoded color values) and rendering dark-on-dark / unreadable in light mode (STYLE-GUIDE.md:25 — both modes first-class). GraceCountdown.tsx is already tokenized and only needs both-modes verification.
narratives.Proposal: Two independent, additive items. (1) Backend: bump both grace-default sites 30->120 (config.ts:289 effective default + boot-reconciliation.ts:14 fallback constant) and update the config.test.ts:142 assertion to 120; the boot-reconciliation.test.ts fixture sets its own graceSeconds mock so it stays self-consistent. (2) Frontend: replace every hardcoded color class in BootReconciliationModal.tsx with the established theme tokens (bg-card/border-border/text-foreground/text-muted-foreground surface; badge-bg-warning+text-warning warning accent per GraceCountdown.tsx:117; badge-bg-success+text-success remote chip; bg-destructive+text-destructive-foreground Freeze button per DialogProvider.tsx:179; bg-primary+text-primary-foreground neutral solid actions; bg-muted+text-muted-foreground chips/unselected toggles), keep the mode-agnostic bg-black/45 scrim, verify GraceCountdown.tsx, and add a static token-guard test asserting no forbidden color classes remain. Confirm legibility with light+dark screenshots. The existing BootReconciliationModal.test.tsx asserts on testids/text (not colors) so it stays green.
narratives.NonGoals:
- No change to boot-reconciliation logic, the candidate-selection rules, or the timer mechanism beyond the default duration.
- No refactor of BootReconciliationModal markup, logic, or testids — colors only.
- No change to GraceCountdown.tsx beyond a residual hardcoded value if one is found (none expected).
- No new config surface or per-project grace override UI.
- No edits to the rendered styleguide-conformance e2e routes; the modal is conditional and not on those routes, so coverage is the static token guard, not the e2e.

autoDecisions.summary: Change BOTH grace-default sites (config.ts:289 and boot-reconciliation.ts:14), not just the constant the issue cites.
autoDecisions.rationale: getBootReconciliationGraceSeconds reads the config value first; DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG.startup.reconciliation_grace_secs=30 always wins over the constant fallback. Changing only the constant leaves the observed default at 30 — a silent no-op. The no-bandaids principle requires fixing the real effective default.

autoDecisions.summary: AC4 coverage = a static token-guard unit test, not an addition to styleguide-conformance.spec.ts.
autoDecisions.rationale: The conformance e2e renders fixed routes (Pipeline/Board/Command Deck/Agents) and never renders BootReconciliationModal, which only mounts when boot reconciliation is pending. A source-scan guard asserting no forbidden color classes is the mechanically-checkable enforcement of AC2 and the realistic 'add coverage' AC4 allows ('if it can target the modal').

autoDecisions.summary: Freeze button uses text-destructive-foreground, not text-white.
autoDecisions.rationale: AC2/FR-2 explicitly forbids text-white. The codebase convention for solid destructive buttons (DialogProvider.tsx:179) is bg-destructive text-destructive-foreground; the style-guide button recipe's literal 'text-white' is superseded by that token convention and would fail the guard test.

autoDecisions.summary: Items 1 and 2 carry no dependency edge.
autoDecisions.rationale: They touch disjoint files (backend constants vs frontend components) with no output->input, shared mutation, or ordering requirement, so they are parallel-safe per the executor edge semantics.

narrative.Action: Bump both grace-default sites from 30 to 120: src/lib/cloister/config.ts:289 (reconciliation_grace_secs: 30 -> 120, inside DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG.startup — the effective default) and src/lib/cloister/boot-reconciliation.ts:14 (DEFAULT_BOOT_RECONCILIATION_GRACE_SECS = 30 -> 120 — the fallback constant). Update tests/lib/cloister/config.test.ts:142 (expect(startup.reconciliation_grace_secs).toBe(30) -> toBe(120)). Re-run src/lib/cloister/__tests__/boot-reconciliation.test.ts; it injects its own graceSeconds mock (:16,:87 set 30 explicitly via the config mock at :32) so it stays self-consistent and needs no change — but confirm no assertion couples a deadline to the default. Do not touch boot-reconciliation logic or the resolver.

items.title: DEFAULT_CLOISTER_CONFIG.startup.reconciliation_grace_secs is 120 and DEFAULT_BOOT_RECONCILIATION_GRACE_SECS is 120; tests/lib/cloister/config.test.ts asserts the default is 120 and passes.

getBootReconciliationGraceSeconds() returns 120 when no override is set, and still returns the override value (e.g. 45) when startup.reconciliation_grace_secs is a finite value > 0 — config override still wins.
The src/lib/cloister/__tests__/boot-reconciliation.test.ts suite passes after the default change to 120.
Restyle BootReconciliationModal to theme tokens + verify GraceCountdown + token guard test (AC2, AC3, AC4)

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/BootReconciliationModal.tsx replace every hardcoded color class (full inventory in the PRD draft .pan/drafts/PAN-2181.md, lines 202/206/209/213/214/220/221/225/226/239/253/254/255/256/258/270/279/286/292/314/330/340/350/360) with theme tokens: surface bg-card + border-border + text-foreground/text-muted-foreground; warning accent badge-bg-warning + badge-border-warning + text-warning (pattern from GraceCountdown.tsx:117-118); remote chip badge-bg-success + text-success; role/read-only chips and unselected toggles bg-muted text-muted-foreground; Resume toggle/Apply solid bg-success text-success-foreground; Keep toggle/Resume-all/Keep-all solid bg-primary text-primary-foreground; Freeze bg-destructive text-destructive-foreground (NOT text-white). Keep the bg-black/45 backdrop-blur-sm scrim and already-tokenized classes. Change colors only — markup, logic, and data-testid values stay identical so BootReconciliationModal.test.tsx stays green. Verify GraceCountdown.tsx is token-clean in both modes (no change expected). Add tests/unit/dashboard/boot-reconciliation-modal-tokens.test.ts that reads both source files and asserts none of the FR-2 forbidden patterns (bg-neutral-*, text-neutral-*, bg-zinc-*, text-zinc-*, text-orange-*, bg-orange-*, border-orange-*, bg-emerald-*, text-emerald-*, bg-sky-*, text-sky-*, bg-red-*, text-red-*, bg-gray-*, text-white, text-black) appear. Implementation checkpoint: capture a screenshot of the modal in light AND dark mode and confirm legibility yourself; fallback is rendering the component under each theme class in an RTL harness and screenshotting that.

items.title: tests/unit/dashboard/boot-reconciliation-modal-tokens.test.ts passes, proving BootReconciliationModal.tsx and GraceCountdown.tsx contain no hardcoded color classes (none of bg-neutral-*, text-neutral-*, bg-zinc-*, text-zinc-*, text-orange-*, bg-orange-*, border-orange-*, bg-emerald-*, text-emerald-*, bg-sky-*, text-sky-*, bg-red-*, text-red-*, bg-gray-*, text-white, text-black) — only theme tokens remain.

The restyled modal renders the Freeze button with bg-destructive text-destructive-foreground (not text-white), the remote chip with the success token (badge-bg-success text-success), and the role/read-only chips with bg-muted text-muted-foreground.
The boot-reconciliation modal renders legibly in light mode and dark mode, shown by a screenshot of the modal captured in each theme.
tests/e2e/styleguide-conformance.spec.ts passes; the new static token-guard test provides the modal-specific coverage AC4 requests.
The existing src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/__tests__/BootReconciliationModal.test.tsx passes unchanged, since its data-testid and text assertions still hold after the color-only restyle.

acceptanceCriteria: AC1: Boot-reconciliation grace countdown defaults to 120 seconds; existing config override still wins.
AC2: BootReconciliationModal.tsx and GraceCountdown.tsx contain no hardcoded color classes — only theme tokens.
AC3: Both components render legibly in light mode and dark mode (verify with a screenshot in each).
AC4: tests/e2e/styleguide-conformance.spec.ts passes; modal coverage added via the static token-guard test.
AC5: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test green.
